scope: Function- and clause-level candidate crosswalk from repository artifacts to NIST AI RMF 1.0 and ISO/IEC 42001:2023. It is not a subcategory audit, Statement of Applicability, or conformity assessment.

sources.note: NIST states that AI RMF 1.0 is voluntary and is being revised; this mapping freezes the named 1.0 functions as of the snapshot date.
The Core organizes outcomes into Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage; NIST says the outcomes are not an ordered checklist.
The official standard page describes requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an AI management system.
The official IEC publication and preview identify the clause 4-10 management-system structure used by this crosswalk.

controls.title: Cross-cutting policies, accountability, and risk governance
controls.limitation: The repository governs public claim wording and technical evidence boundaries; it does not establish an organization's approved AI policies, risk tolerance, legal obligations, resources, or accountable executives.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Inspect the named local gate and documents only as software-project evidence. Obtain organizational records and role-owner attestations separately.

controls.title: Context, actors, impacts, and risk identification
controls.limitation: Architecture and threat-boundary documents describe the reference implementation, not a complete use-case inventory, affected-party analysis, impact assessment, deployment context, or empirically observed harm profile.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Treat these artifacts as inputs to context mapping, not completion of MAP outcomes for any deploying organization.

controls.title: Assessment, monitoring, and documented measurement
controls.limitation: Local schemas, fixtures, receipt checks, and correlation mechanics do not establish guardrail validity, representative sampling, calibrated thresholds, live performance, causal dependence, or independent evaluation.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Reproduce the local tests and separately assess measurement validity, data quality, uncertainty, and deployment monitoring.

controls.title: Risk prioritization, treatment, response, and improvement
controls.limitation: Deterministic local enforcement and documented workflows do not show an operating review team, accepted risk-treatment decisions, executed incident response, recovery, or continuous monitoring.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Require live operating records, named owners, service-level objectives, incident exercises, and deployment-specific risk acceptance before crediting organizational outcomes.

controls.title: Context of the organization
controls.limitation: Repository scope and architecture are documented, but no organization has approved an AIMS scope or supplied its internal/external context and interested-party requirements.
controls.reviewer_caveat: A deploying organization must define and approve its own scope; the reference implementation cannot do so on its behalf.

controls.title: Leadership
controls.limitation: Technical governance documents are not evidence of leadership commitment, an approved AI policy, delegated authorities, or management accountability.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Obtain signed organizational policy, responsibility assignments, leadership review records, and authority evidence outside this repository.

controls.title: Planning
controls.limitation: The project tracks technical evidence risks and future work; it does not contain an organization-approved AI risk assessment methodology, impact assessment, objectives, treatment plan, or controlled plan for organizational change.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Do not convert roadmap or engineering-risk entries into organizational risk acceptance without accountable review.

controls.title: Support
controls.limitation: Reviewer instructions and contribution rules do not prove adequate resources, competence, awareness, communications, or controlled organizational records.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Assess training, competence, resourcing, communication, retention, approval, and document-control systems separately.

controls.title: Operation
controls.limitation: Local enforcement tests and a deployment runbook do not evidence operational planning, deployed risk treatment, or completed impact assessment in a named environment.
controls.reviewer_caveat: A bounded live bundle can support only the named run and controls; it cannot establish organization-wide conformity.

controls.title: Performance evaluation
controls.limitation: Automated validation and an external-review guide are not an internal audit programme, management review, monitoring plan, or evaluated organizational objective.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Require audit independence, audit scope/results, management-review inputs/decisions, metrics, and follow-up records.

controls.title: Improvement
controls.limitation: The repository defines incident and corrective-action artifact shapes, but contains no organizational nonconformity records, root-cause evidence, effectiveness review, or continual-improvement decisions.
controls.reviewer_caveat: Schema-valid examples are simulations; inspect executed corrective actions and management decisions separately.

non_claims: This candidate crosswalk does not certify or demonstrate conformity with NIST AI RMF or ISO/IEC 42001.
Repository evidence cannot replace organization-specific scope, legal analysis, risk acceptance, operational records, internal audit, management review, or accredited conformity assessment.
NIST states that AI RMF 1.0 is voluntary and under revision; mappings must be re-reviewed when the framework changes.
